rationale: Falsifiability/AC lens. Core ask passes cleanly: all nine ACs are structural (test -f / grep / go test / go build); none secretly needs live agents. Every live-agent behavior is quarantined in the single Validation Proof explicitly labeled Manual/not-CI, and there is no dry-run AC pretending to be automatable — the honest split ADR-0040 demands. I verified each structural AC is runnable: the referenced functions/patterns (attributeDomain, isDocFile/isProcessArtifact, TestWorkflowOwnsTraceAndGolangci, SkillFiles, installSkills) all exist on the base; every skill section the deletion-guard greps assert (Slot lens defaults, Launch the panel, Artifact gates, Consolidate) is currently present; AC2's OWNERSHIP regex matches the actual YAML form; the embed/install/byte-identical/claim/attributeDomain chain (R8/R9) is well covered. 109/110 legitimately absent — consistent with the stated rebase-forward-before-plan-approve ordering. Gaps are AC-completeness, not spec-defeating: R3's parse-failure re-prompt branch is falsified nowhere; AC4's negative grep is comment-brittle; AC8 pins 2 of 4 named tally sections; 'verbatim' is stronger than the manual proof's 'contains'. Fix as concrete changes at plan time. Not a block.
concrete_changes_required: R3 parse-failure branch is unfalsified: neither the structural ACs nor the Manual proof exercises a malformed-verdict (non-quota) recovery, yet R3's headline benefit is 'removes the verdict-parse failure class.' Add a Manual-proof bullet: deliberately malformed codex stdout -> wrapper re-prompts once -> (still bad) -> substitute/leave-missing. Also clarify whether a parse-failure substitution reuses R4's reviewer_id 'claude-sub' (which would mislabel a non-quota substitution) or a distinct marker.

AC4's `! grep -q 'mindspec complete'` is defeated by any descriptive comment in the workflow that mentions the string (e.g. `// never runs mindspec complete`), failing the check despite correct behavior; symmetrically the positive verb greps can be satisfied by a comment, not a real call. Anchor the greps to a call-shaped pattern, or state that the workflow must not contain the literal `mindspec complete` in any form (comments included).
AC8 pins only 2 of the 4 tally judgment sections R7 declares 'unchanged'. Add greps for 'After a halt' and 'Escape hatch' so all four survival guards are enforced.

R5 / ADR-0035 assert the workflow result carries verify/tally output 'verbatim', but the Manual proof only checks it 'contains' the reports. Either weaken the prose to 'contains the verify/tally output' or add a manual check that the surfaced text is the CLI's pass-through (recovery lines identical), since an agent step running the verb could summarize.

findings.text: The wrapper 're-prompts once or substitutes on a parse failure' rule has zero falsification: no structural AC pins it, and the Manual proof exercises only the quota-wall branch (R4), not a malformed-verdict recovery. The 'once' bound and the substitution-vs-reprompt choice are also under-specified. Add it to the Manual proof and disambiguate the reviewer_id labeling for a parse-failure substitution.

AC4's negative `! grep -q 'mindspec complete'` is comment-brittle — a workflow comment mentioning the forbidden string would fail the AC despite correct behavior; positive verb greps are symmetrically satisfiable by comments rather than real agent-step calls. This is a grep-AC brittleness, not a design flaw.

AC8 asserts survival of only 2 of the 4 tally sections R7 names as unchanged (Consolidate, Artifact gates); 'After a halt — recovery' and 'Escape hatch' survival is unpinned. Both sections exist in the current SKILL.md, so the guard is cheap to complete.

The 'verbatim' claim (R5 body + ADR-0035 touchpoint) is stronger than the Manual proof's 'result contains the verify/tally reports.' Verbatim pass-through vs agent-summarization is not falsified; tighten either the prose or the proof.

Consistency risk to re-check at rebase: the cited '110 contract path' <spec-dir>/reviews/<slug>/<slot>-round-<N>.json differs from the current operational panel dir convention review/<slug>/. Spec 110 is absent from this base, so I cannot falsify that 110 actually defines <spec-dir>/reviews/...; verify at the post-109/110 rebase that the path 111 hard-codes matches 110's schema.

Strength, not a defect: R1's 'script never execs CLI / writes a file', R2's 'no hand-written panel.json', and R5's 'no consolidated-round-<N>.md' are design invariants enforced by the documented workflow limit and reviewer inspection, with no grep AC. Acceptable — but note these three negatives rest on review + the Manual proof, not CI, so a plan-time reviewer must confirm them by reading the JS.

The live-LLM boundary is drawn honestly: all 9 ACs are structural, and every agent-dependent behavior (fan-out execution, codex-never-writes, both substitution branches, result-carries-verify/tally) is confined to the single explicitly-marked Manual proof. No AC smuggles a live-agent check into the automatable list, and there is no dry-run AC pretending to run agent-free. This is the correct portability posture.
